© Lab Technician

Lab Technician Landing Page

A minimal single-page landing site for a lab technician. The page is responsive, uses a red/black/white color scheme, includes a hero with title and subtitle, a brief services list, and a contact form that shows an alert on submit (no backend).

Features
- Single HTML page
- Responsive mobile-first layout (usable from ~320px to desktop widths)
- Hero section with title and subtitle
- Services list
- Contact form (name, email, message) that triggers an alert on submit
- Color palette limited to red (#ff0000), black (#000000), and white (#ffffff)
- Basic accessibility: labeled form inputs, logical heading structure (h1 > h2), visible focus states, sufficient color contrast for body text

Getting Started
- Open index.html in your browser.
- Click "Contact Me" to jump to the form. Submitting the form displays a simple alert and resets the fields.

Testing
- Requires Node.js (LTS recommended).
- Install dependencies (only Jest is required for the test):
  npm install
- Run tests:
  npm test

Project Structure
- index.html: Single-page content
- styles.css: Responsive styles using only red, black, and white
- script.js: Contact form alert behavior and footer year
- tests/hero-title.test.js: Jest test to ensure the hero title exists

Notes on Accessibility & Contrast
- Body text uses black on white (21:1 contrast).
- Red is used as an accent (borders, interactive states). When red is used as a background (e.g., CTA hover), text remains black to maintain sufficient contrast on red.
- Form inputs have associated labels via for/id attributes. Focus styles are visible and high-contrast.

notes: Manual checks: At ~320px width, content stacks with no horizontal scroll; focus styles are visible; labels are linked to inputs via for/id; body text uses black on white for maximum contrast; interactive hover on red keeps black text to meet contrast.
Run tests with npm test; open index.html directly in a browser to interact with the page.
